XOnlineCompetitionCheckin

Enables a player to perform the required check-in for a competition event. Check-in must succeed prior to starting any round.

HRESULT XOnlineCompetitionCheckin(
    DWORD dwUserIndex,
    DWORD dwTemplate,
    ULONGLONG qwTeamID,
    ULONGLONG qwCompetitionID,
    ULONGLONG qwEventID,
    HANDLE hWorkEvent,
    PXONLINETASK_HANDLE phTask
);

Parameters

dwUserIndex
[in] Controller index (zero-based) of the player. The player must have been successfully logged on with a previous call to XOnlineLogon.
dwTemplate
[in] ID of a template describing the competition.
qwTeamID
[in] Team ID. The value in this parameter is used only if this is a team competition rather than a competition between individuals. If this is not a team competition, set this value to zero.
qwCompetitionID
[in] Unique ID that specifies the competition.
qwEventID
[in] ID of the current event within the competition. This ID is received from the topology query.
hWorkEvent
[in, optional] Handle of the event you want to associate with this task. If you do not want to assign an event, set this parameter to NULL.
phTask
[out] Pointer to an XONLINETASK_HANDLE type. The handle is associated with the task started by XOnlineCompetitionCheckin. The handle can then be used in calls to XOnlineTaskContinue to perform work associated with this task.

Return Values

Returns S_OK if successful, or an error code otherwise. The error codes can include those listed in the following table.

Error Code Description
XONLINE_E_QUERY_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND The specified competition does not exist.
XONLINE_E_COMP_CHECKIN_TOO_EARLY Check-in for the competition event has not started yet.
XONLINE_E_COMP_CANCELLED The competition has been canceled.
XONLINE_E_COMP_CHECKIN_BAD_EVENT The player is not a valid participant in the event, or the event does not exist.
XONLINE_E_COMP_CHECKIN_TIME_INVALID The player is attempting to check in to an event after the round has ended.
XONLINE_E_COMP_CHECKIN_EVENT_SCORED The player is attempting to check in to an event that has already been scored by the service. Most likely the player has already forfeited or been ejected.
XONLINE_S_COMP_CHECKIN_EVENT_SCORED The entrant's opponent in the event forfeited or has been ejected, so the entrant has automatically won the event by checking in. Re-query topology to view results.

Remarks

Titles call XOnlineCompetitionCheckin when a player is ready to play a competition event, reguardless of whether the match opponent is online and ready to play. A successful call to XOnlineCompetitionCheckin should be followed by a game invitation to the player's opponent in the event.

Requirements

Header: Declared in Xonline.h.

Import Library: Use Xonline.lib.

See Also

XOnlineCompetitionCreate, XOnlineCompetitionTopology, XOnlineCompetitionTopologySingleElimination
